name: Les Oreilles De Cochon (Pigs' Ears)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift the flour, salt, and sugar together.
2. Rub in the butter with your fingertips until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs, as for pie dough.
3. Beat the eggs with water and vinegar in a bowl.
4. Add the egg mixture to the flour mixture and stir until well combined.
5. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead it 2-3 minutes until smooth.
6. Chill the dough for 30 minutes, then divide into 12 pieces.
7. Roll each piece out very thin, like pasta dough, into rough 5-inch squares.
8. Cut each square in half on the diagonal.
9. Heat about 1/2 inch of shortening or lard to 360°F (180°C) in a frying pan.
10. Fry the pieces in single layers until golden brown, turning once.
11. In a saucepan, combine the syrup and sugar. Cook over low heat until it reaches the soft crack stage, about 270°F (130°C).
12. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
13. Drain the fried pastries on paper towels.
14. Drizzle about 1 tablespoon of the syrup over each pastry.
15. Serve immediately.
